creator: imriz@co.zahav.net.il
text:
Hi Simon,

I've noticed it was MFC'ed a couple of minutes after I've submitted the
bug followup. I am now running 6.2-STABLE with that patch. Seems quite
stable so far, but during the weekend the machine is not under the same
load, so I guess I will have to wait and see if this patch solved it. I
will update the PR with my findings next week.


Thanks!

-- Imri

-----Original Message-----
From: Simon L. Nielsen [mailto:simon@FreeBSD.org]=20
Sent: Friday, January 19, 2007 1:19 AM
To: Imri Zvik
Cc: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
Subject: Re: kern/107325: [panic] 6-STABLE panic, possibly UNIX domain
sockets related

On 2007.01.19 00:42:00 +0200, Imri Zvik wrote:
> Hi,
>=20
> A very similar panic happened to me 3 times in the last 24 hours:
>=20
[...]

> It happened to me on 6.2-PRERELEASE, and then I've upgraded to 6.2
> RELEASE:
>=20
> FreeBSD cemetery 6.2-RELEASE FreeBSD 6.2-RELEASE #4: Thu Jan 18
00:01:29
> IST 2007     root@cemetery:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/IGLD  i386
>=20
> Is it possible to get the patch mentioned in PR? I will gladly apply
it
> and test it (the panic happened on a very busy mail server, running
> postfix) here.

The patch has been MFC'ed to RELENG_6 so you can either find the
particular commit by jhb and generate a patch yourself or just use
RELENG_6.  The commit to head mentioned mx1 in the commit log, AFAIR
the MFC didn't.

I think it's likely there will be an errata notice which merges this
to RELENG_6_2, but it's up to re@ and if it's going to happen it will
probably be a couple of weeks at least before this happens.

--=20
Simon L. Nielsen
